# Sprint Specifications — Senior Dev Adversarial Review Handoff

**Generated:** 2026-03-17
**Generated By:** Planning Agent (5 parallel Opus 4.6 sub-agents)
**Review Requested:** Adversarial review by Senior Dev agent
**Status:** Reconciled draft — use for re-review after the 2026-03-17 contract fixes

> **Review artifact notice:** This file is a reviewer handoff, not an implementation source of truth. For planning authority, start with `Category 3 - IA West Smart Match CRM/docs/SPRINT_PLAN.md` and `PRD_SECTION_CAT3.md`. Use `Category 3 - IA West Smart Match CRM/PLAN.md` only as background context.

---

## Your Mission

You are a **Senior Dev acting as an adversarial reviewer** for 5 sprint specification files that will be handed to implementation agents. Your job is to find every flaw, gap, contradiction, and unrealistic assumption before code gets written. Think of yourself as the last line of defense — once these specs ship, implementation agents will follow them literally.

**Review mindset:** Assume the implementation agent is competent but literal. It will do exactly what the spec says. If the spec is wrong, the agent builds the wrong thing. If the spec is ambiguous, the agent makes an arbitrary choice. If the spec has gaps, the agent skips that work entirely.

---

## Canonical Upstream Documents (Read These First)

Read in this order:

1. **`Category 3 - IA West Smart Match CRM/docs/SPRINT_PLAN.md`** — Canonical execution plan for scope, sprint tasks, milestone gates, and day-by-day expectations
2. **`PRD_SECTION_CAT3.md`** — Canonical feature-detail narrative, tech stack, risk analysis, and dataset inventory

5. **`Category 3 - IA West Smart Match CRM/PLAN.md`** — Background-only architecture/rationale. Consult when useful, but do not treat as planning authority.

## Reconciled Contracts To Re-Verify

These were the 2026-03-17 reconciliation targets. They should now be clean in the sprint specs. The reviewer should verify that no regression remains and still look for anything else the automated check missed.

| Area | Expected Current Contract |
|---|---|
| Source of truth | `SPRINT_PLAN.md` owns execution, `PRD_SECTION_CAT3.md` owns feature detail, portfolio context comes from `archived/general_project_docs/MASTER_SPRINT_PLAN.md` and `archived/general_project_docs/STRATEGIC_REVIEW.md`, and `PLAN.md` is background-only |
| Imports + launch | `from src...` imports throughout and `streamlit run src/app.py` as the supported launch command |
| Embeddings | Sprint 0 writes flat artifacts under `cache/`; Sprint 1 reads those same flat artifacts; 68 rows are embedded, 9 calendar rows are loaded but not embedded |
| Match schema | Downstream consumers use Sprint 1's canonical keys: `total_score` plus `factor_scores.{topic_relevance, role_fit, geographic_proximity, calendar_fit, historical_conversion, student_interest}` |
| Event schema | CSV-backed events use literal raw headers such as `Event / Program`, `Host / Unit`, and `Volunteer Roles (fit)`; discovery events may use normalized keys like `event_name` and `volunteer_roles`; adapters must support both |
| Scrape cache | Scraped pages are cached under `cache/scrapes/<sha256(url)>.json` |
| Email cache | Generated outreach emails are cached under `cache/emails/` using the same runtime helper the app uses |
| Cloud scrape behavior | Sprint 4 must preserve Sprint 2's `scrape_university()` dict return shape even when the cloud path serves cached artifacts only |
| Custom URL safety | Custom URL flow must reject non-`http/https` schemes, localhost, private-network targets, and non-university public hosts |

---

## Adversarial Review Checklist

### Pass 1: Spec-to-Upstream Fidelity

For each sprint file, verify against the canonical upstreams:

- [ ] **Task coverage:** Every task ID in `SPRINT_PLAN.md` (A0.1-A0.7, A1.1-A1.7, A2.1-A2.7, B2.1-B2.6, A3.1-A3.7, B3.1-B3.6, A4.1-A4.10, B4.1-B4.9) has a corresponding section in the sprint spec
- [ ] **Hour budgets match:** Sprint spec hours per task match `SPRINT_PLAN.md`
- [ ] **Definition of Done items match:** Sprint spec DoD checkboxes match `SPRINT_PLAN.md` DoD
- [ ] **Go/No-Go gates match:** Pass/Fail criteria match `SPRINT_PLAN.md`
- [ ] **Tech stack compliance:** All technologies used are within the approved stack from `PRD_SECTION_CAT3.md`
- [ ] **MATCH_SCORE formula matches:** 6 factors, default weights (0.30/0.25/0.20/0.15/0.05/0.05) match `Category 3 - IA West Smart Match CRM/docs/SPRINT_PLAN.md` and `PRD_SECTION_CAT3.md` (`PLAN.md` may be consulted for background rationale only)
- [ ] **Data file schemas match:** Column names and row counts match actual CSV files
- [ ] **University targets match:** UCLA, SDSU, UC Davis, USC, Portland State per `PRD_SECTION_CAT3.md`

### Pass 2: Cross-Sprint Consistency (Interfaces)

At every sprint boundary, verify:

- [ ] **Function signatures match:** Functions defined in Sprint N are called correctly in Sprint N+1
- [ ] **File paths match:** Modules created in Sprint N are imported correctly in Sprint N+1
- [ ] **Data formats match:** Output schemas in Sprint N match input expectations in Sprint N+1
- [ ] **Cache structures match:** Cache files written in Sprint N are read correctly in Sprint N+1
- [ ] **Session state keys match:** `st.session_state` keys set in one sprint are read correctly in later sprints
- [ ] **Config constants match:** Constants defined in `src/config.py` are referenced consistently everywhere

### Pass 3: Implementability (Can a Code Agent Build This?)

For each task specification:

- [ ] **Function signatures are complete:** All parameters have types, all return types specified
- [ ] **File paths are unambiguous:** Every module has exactly one specified location
- [ ] **Dependencies are explicit:** Every import, library, and API is listed
- [ ] **Edge cases are specified:** What happens on empty input, API failure, missing data, malformed HTML?
- [ ] **UI components have wireframes:** ASCII layout or description sufficient to build from
- [ ] **Prompt templates are complete:** LLM prompts include system message, few-shot examples, output format
- [ ] **Test criteria are measurable:** Acceptance criteria are verifiable (not "works correctly" but "returns score > 0.15 for known-good pair")
- [ ] **No circular dependencies:** Module A doesn't import B which imports A

### Pass 4: Adversarial Attack Vectors

Stress-test the specs for failure modes:

- [ ] **What breaks if Gemini API is unreachable?** Is every API call wrapped with fallback?
- [ ] **What breaks if a CSV column is renamed?** Are column names hardcoded or configurable?
- [ ] **What breaks if Streamlit Cloud has 1GB RAM?** Are all DataFrames small enough?
- [ ] **What breaks if a university blocks scraping?** Does every scrape have a cached fallback?
- [ ] **What breaks if demo mode is ON but fixtures are missing?** Is there a fixture validation step?
- [ ] **What breaks if two speakers have identical names?** Are speaker IDs unique?
- [ ] **What breaks if an event has zero volunteer roles?** Is the role_fit factor safe?
- [ ] **What breaks if all weights are set to 0?** Is there a minimum-weight guard?
- [ ] **What breaks if the .ics date field is unparseable?** Is there a date parsing fallback?
- [ ] **What breaks if Playwright is unavailable on Streamlit Cloud?** Is there a bs4-only fallback?
- [ ] **What breaks if Gemini returns malformed JSON?** Is there JSON parsing with retry?
- [ ] **What breaks if the user enters a malicious URL in Custom URL?** Is there input validation / SSRF prevention?

### Pass 5: Hour Budget Realism

- [ ] **Sprint 0 (14-16h / 2 days):** Is 1.5h realistic for env setup with Playwright? Is 2.5h enough for embedding pipeline including API integration and caching?
- [ ] **Sprint 1 (21-24h / 3 days):** Is 5h enough for the Matches tab UI with radar charts, sliders, and explanation cards? Is 4h enough for the 6-factor MATCH_SCORE with all edge cases?
- [ ] **Sprint 2 (21-24h + 18-24h / 3 days):** Is 5h enough for web scraping across 5 universities with both bs4 and Playwright? Is the Track B timeline realistic for 4h research + 7h drafting in 3 days?
- [ ] **Sprint 3 (14-16h + 12-16h / 2 days):** Can feature freeze realistically happen on Day 9 if Sprint 2 runs to Day 8? Is 0.5h enough for screenshot export?
- [ ] **Sprint 4 (28-32h + 18-24h / 4 days):** Is 4h enough for E2E testing of the entire app? Is 2h enough for backup video recording + editing?

### Pass 6: Written Deliverables Quality

Sprint 2-4 Track B tasks produce the written deliverables worth **40% of judging**:

- [ ] **Growth Strategy:** Does the spec provide enough structure for a compelling 2.5-3 page document? Are real data integration points specified?
- [ ] **Measurement Plan:** Are the 7 KPIs actually measurable with available data? Is the A/B test proposal statistically sound?
- [ ] **Responsible AI Note:** Are the mitigations concrete and specific (not generic "we will address bias")?
- [ ] **Demo Script:** Does the 5-minute timing add up? Are backup plans specified for every failure mode?

---

## Review Output Format

Please produce your review as a structured report:

```markdown
# Sprint Spec Adversarial Review — [Date]

## Executive Summary
[2-3 sentences: overall quality assessment, critical blockers, recommendation (approve/revise/reject)]

## Critical Findings (Must Fix Before Implementation)
| # | Sprint | Task | Finding | Recommended Fix | Impact if Unfixed |
|---|--------|------|---------|-----------------|-------------------|

## High-Priority Findings (Should Fix)
| # | Sprint | Task | Finding | Recommended Fix |
|---|--------|------|---------|-----------------|

## Medium-Priority Findings (Nice to Fix)
| # | Sprint | Task | Finding | Recommended Fix |
|---|--------|------|---------|-----------------|

## Low-Priority / Stylistic
[Bullet list]

## Known Issues Confirmation
[Confirm/deny each of the 9 pre-identified issues, note any that are actually non-issues]

## Hour Budget Assessment
[Per-sprint assessment: realistic / tight / unrealistic, with specific callouts]

## Missing Specifications
[Anything the specs should cover but don't]

## Verdict
- [ ] APPROVED — Ready for implementation agents
- [ ] APPROVED WITH REQUIRED FIXES — Fix critical findings, then proceed
- [ ] REVISE — Significant rework needed before implementation
- [ ] REJECT — Fundamental issues require re-planning
```

---

## How to Run This Review

### Option A: Single Deep-Dive Agent (Recommended)

```
Prompt: "You are a Senior Dev performing an adversarial review of 5 sprint
specification files. Read REVIEW_HANDOFF.md for your full instructions,
then read all 5 sprint files and the 3 canonical upstream documents.
Produce the review report as specified in the handoff."
```

Use Opus 4.6 for maximum reasoning depth. Expected runtime: 10-15 minutes. Expected context: ~50K tokens (12K sprint lines + 3K upstream docs + review framework).

### Option B: Parallel Review Panels (Faster, More Thorough)

Launch 3 parallel agents with different review lenses:

1. **Technical Correctness Agent:** Passes 1-3 (fidelity, consistency, implementability)
2. **Adversarial/Security Agent:** Pass 4 (attack vectors, failure modes, edge cases)
3. **Product/Strategy Agent:** Passes 5-6 (hour budgets, deliverables quality, demo readiness)

Merge findings into a single report afterward.

### Option C: Red Team / Blue Team

1. **Red Team Agent:** Try to find specs that would produce broken, insecure, or failing code
2. **Blue Team Agent:** Defend the specs and propose minimal fixes for Red Team findings

---

## Context for the Reviewer

### What These Specs Are
- **12,273 lines** of implementation-ready sprint specifications
- Generated by **5 parallel Opus 4.6 sub-agents**, each producing one sprint file independently
- Cover a **14-day dual-track hackathon prep** for IA West SmartMatch CRM (Category 3)
- Target: **April 16, 2026 hackathon** at Cal Poly Pomona

### What These Specs Contain Per Task
- Full function signatures with types
- File paths and module locations
- API contracts and prompt templates
- ASCII UI wireframes
- Acceptance criteria (checkbox format)
- Harness guidelines (code structure rules for implementation agents)
- Steer guidelines (edge case handling, error recovery, design decisions)

### Why Adversarial Review Matters
These specs will be consumed by **implementation agents** that follow them literally. Every ambiguity becomes an arbitrary decision. Every contradiction becomes a bug. Every gap becomes missing functionality. The cost of fixing specs now is ~10 minutes of review. The cost of fixing bugs in implementation is ~10x that.

### Known Architectural Decisions
- **Streamlit** for frontend (rapid prototyping, free cloud hosting)
- **CSV + Pandas** for data (no database — hackathon scope)
- **Gemini `gemini-embedding-001`** for vector matching (1536 dimensions)
- **Gemini** for LLM tasks (extraction, email gen, explanations)
- **BeautifulSoup + Playwright** for web scraping (static + dynamic pages)
- **Session state** for runtime data (no persistence beyond CSV append)
- **Demo mode** with cached fixtures as backup for live demo

### Judging Criteria (What Matters Most)
| Criterion | Points | Weight |
|-----------|--------|--------|
| Prototype Quality | ~15 | 30% |
| Growth Strategy | ~10 | 20% |
| Measurement Plan | ~10 | 20% |
| Responsible AI | ~10 | 20% |
| Scalability | ~5 | 10% |

Written deliverables = 40% of score. The specs must support both code AND writing tracks.

---

## Code Review Resolution (2026-03-19)

All 17 findings from the adversarial code review have been resolved:

### CRITICAL (3)

| ID | File | Finding | Resolution |
|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | scraper.py | `datetime.fromisoformat()` crash on corrupt cache | Wrapped in `try/except (ValueError, KeyError)`, added naive→UTC backward compat, replaced `datetime.utcnow()` with `datetime.now(UTC)` |
| C2 | scraper.py | SSRF TOCTOU gap between DNS validation and HTTP request | Extracted `_resolve_validated_ips()`, added `_validated_scrape_bs4()` with DNS pinning via `socket.getaddrinfo` override |
| C3 | llm_extractor.py | Prompt injection via content delimiters | Changed delimiters to `<content>`/`</content>` XML tags, added `_sanitize_for_prompt()` escaping |

### HIGH (7)

| ID | File | Finding | Resolution |
|---|---|---|---|
| H1 | scraper.py | Thread-unsafe rate limiter (shared mutable `_last_request_time`) | Added `threading.Lock` around read-check-sleep-write block |
| H2 | matches_tab.py | Session state mutation inside slider loop | Collect slider values into `updated_weights` dict, assign once after loop |
| H3 | matches_tab.py | `.ics` generation on every Streamlit rerender | Moved import to top-of-file, added `@st.cache_data` wrapper `_cached_generate_ics()` |
| H4 | discovery_tab.py | DNS lookup on every keystroke in Custom URL field | Added `@st.cache_data(ttl=300)` wrapper `_cached_validate_custom_url()` |
| H5 | email_gen.py + email_panel.py | Private function `_event_value` imported cross-module | Renamed to `event_value` (public API) |
| H6 | ics_generator.py | DTSTART/DTEND missing timezone designator | Appended `Z` to format strings, fixed `_parse_date` fallback to use `datetime.now(UTC)` |
| H7 | test_engine.py | Flaky test with only 50 samples | Increased to 100 entries with descriptive assertion message |

### MEDIUM (7)

| ID | File | Finding | Resolution |
|---|---|---|---|
| M1 | embeddings.py | `assert` used for runtime validation | Replaced with `if ... raise ValueError(...)` |
| M2 | matches_tab.py | Speaker name in Streamlit widget keys (non-URL-safe) | Replaced with rank index (unique per render cycle) |
| M3 | pipeline_tab.py | Bare `except Exception` swallows all errors | Narrowed to `(FileNotFoundError, pd.errors.ParserError, pd.errors.EmptyDataError, OSError)` with `exc` logging |
| M4 | factors.py | `canonical_regions` set recomputed on every call | Moved to module-level `_CANONICAL_REGIONS: frozenset[str]` |
| M5 | factors.py | `pd.isna()` doesn't catch string `"nan"` | Added explicit `str(...).strip().lower() == "nan"` guard |
| M6 | email_gen.py | Confusing `EMAIL_CACHE_DIR` re-assignment | Replaced comment with: "Intentionally shadows config import; tests patch this attribute." |
| M7 | engine.py | Ambiguous cumulative vs per-stage pipeline semantics | Added cross-reference comments between `_PIPELINE_STAGES` and `stage_transitions` |
